title: merremia tridentata subsp. angustifolia
intro: Merremia tridentata subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and it is also used in some herbal preparations, such as teas and ointments.
